content.title: How much higher? How much faster?

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Since the early years of the twentieth century, when the International Athletic Federation began keeping records, there has been a steady improvement in how fast athletes run, how high they jump and how far they are able to hurl massive objects, themselves included, through space. For the so-called power events—that require a relatively brief, explosive release of energy, like the 100-metre sprint and the long jump—times and distances have improved ten to twenty percent. In the endurance events the results have been more dramatic. At the 1908 Olympics, John Hayes of the U.S. team ran the marathon in a time of 2:55:18. In 1999, Morocco's Khalid Khannouchi set a new world record of 2:05:42, almost thirty percent faster.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: No one theory can explain improvements in performance, but the most important factor has been genetics. "The athlete must choose his parents carefully," says Jesus Dapena, a sports scientist at Indiana University, invoking an oft-cited adage. Over the past century, the composition of the human gene pool has not changed appreciably, but with increasing global participation in athletics—and greater rewards to tempt athletes—it is more likely that individuals possessing the unique complement of genes for athletic performance can be identified early. "Was there someone like [sprinter] Michael Johnson in the 1920s?" Dapena asks. "I'm sure there was, but his talent was probably never realized."

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Identifying genetically talented individuals is only the first step. Michael Yessis, an emeritus professor of Sports Science at California State University at Fullerton, maintains that "genetics only determines about one third of what an athlete can do. But with the right training we can go much further with that one third than we've been going." Yessis believes that U.S. runners, despite their impressive achievements, are "running on their genetics". By applying more scientific methods, "they're going to go much faster". These methods include strength training that duplicates what they are doing in their running events as well as plyometrics, a technique pioneered in the former Soviet Union.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Whereas most exercises are designed to build up strength or endurance, plyometrics focuses on increasing power—the rate at which an athlete can expend energy. When a sprinter runs, Yessis explains, her foot stays in contact with the ground for just under a tenth of a second, half of which is devoted to landing and the other half to pushing off. Plyometric exercises help athletes make the best use of this brief interval.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Nutrition is another area that sports trainers have failed to address adequately. "Many athletes are not getting the best nutrition, even though supplements," Yessis insists. Each activity has its own nutritional needs. Few coaches, for instance, understand how deficiencies in trace minerals can lead to injuries.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Focused training will also play a role in enabling records to be broken. "If we applied the Russian training model to some of the outstanding runners we have in this country," Yessis asserts, "they would be breaking records left and right." He will not predict by how much, however: "Exactly what the limits are it's hard to say, but there will be increases even if only by hundredths of a second, as long as our training continues to improve."

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: One of the most important new methodologies is biomechanics, the study of the body in motion. A biomechanic films an athlete in action and then digitizes her performance, recording the motion of every joint and limb in three dimensions. By applying Newton's laws to these motions, "we can say that this athlete's run is not fast enough; that this one is not using his arms strongly enough during take-off," says Dapena, who uses these methods to help high jumpers. To date, however, biomechanics has made only a small difference to athletic performance.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: Revolutionary ideas still come from the athletes themselves. For example, during the 1968 Olympics in Mexico City, a relatively unknown high jumper named Dick Fosbury won the gold by going over the bar backwards, in complete contradiction of all the received high-jumping wisdom, a move instantly dubbed the Fosbury flop. Fosbury himself did not know what he was doing. That understanding took the later analysis of biomechanics specialists who put their minds to comprehending something that was too complex and unorthodox ever to have been invented through their own mathematical simulations. Fosbury also required another element that lies behind many improvements in athletic performance: an innovation in athletic equipment. In Fosbury's case, it was the cushions that jumpers land on. Traditionally, high jumpers would land in pits filled with sawdust. But by Fosbury's time, sawdust pits had been replaced by soft foam cushions, ideal for flopping.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: In the end, most people who examine human performance are humbled by the resourcefulness of athletes and the powers of the human body. "Once you study athletics, you learn that it's a vexingly complex issue," says John S. Raglin, a sports psychologist at Indiana University. "Core performance is not a simple or mundane thing of higher, faster, longer. So many variables enter into the equation, and our understanding in many cases is fundamental. We've got a long way to go." For the foreseeable future, records will be made to be broken.

questions.title: Modern official athletic records date from about 1900.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "Modern official athletic records date from about 1900" 对应原文中的 "Since the early years of the twentieth century, when the International Athletic Federation began keeping records"。这里 "early years of the twentieth century" 和 "about 1900" 是同义表达，而 "International Athletic Federation began keeping records" 对应 "official athletic records"。因此，题干信息在原文中可以找到，答案为 TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Since the early years of the twentieth century, when the International Athletic Federation began keeping records, there has been a steady improvement in how fast athletes run, how high they jump and how far they are able to hurl massive objects, themselves included, through space.
central_sentences.translation: 自二十世纪初期国际田径联合会开始保存记录以来，运动员跑得更快、跳得更高、投掷重物（包括他们自己）的距离也越来越远。

questions.title: There was little improvement in athletic performance before the twentieth century.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词 "little improvement" 和 "before the twentieth century" 在原文中没有直接对应的表述。原文提到的是二十世纪初期以来的改进情况，但没有提到二十世纪之前的情况。因此，无法确定题干中的陈述是否正确，答案应为 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: Since the early years of the twentieth century, when the International Athletic Federation began keeping records, there has been a steady improvement in how fast athletes run, how high they jump and how far they are able to hurl massive objects, themselves included, through space.

questions.title: Performance has improved most greatly in events requiring an intensive burst of energy.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "intensive burst of energy" 对应原文中的 "brief, explosive release of energy"，而 "improved most greatly" 对应 "more dramatic"。原文明确指出，力量项目的成绩提高了10%到20%，而耐力项目的结果更加显著。因此，题干所述的力量项目成绩提高最大是错误的，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: For the so-called power events -that require a relatively brief, explosive release of energy, like the 100-metre sprint and the long jump-times and distances have improved ten to twenty percent. In the endurance events the results have been more dramatic.
central_sentences.translation: 对于所谓的力量项目——需要相对短暂、爆发性能量释放的项目，如100米短跑和跳远——时间和距离提高了10%到20%。在耐力项目中，结果更加显著。

In the endurance events the results have been more dramatic.

questions.title: Improvements in athletic performance can be fully explained by genetics.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“fully explained”意思是“完全解释”，而原文明确指出“没有一种理论可以解释运动表现的提高”，这表明遗传学并不能完全解释运动表现的提高。因此，题干的陈述是错误的，正确答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: No one theory can explain improvements in performance, but the most important factor has been genetics.
central_sentences.translation: 没有一种理论可以解释运动表现的提高，但最重要的因素是遗传学。

target_sen.target_sen: No one theory can explain improvements in performance, but the most important factor has been genetics.
No one theory can explain improvements in performance, but the most important factor has been genetics.

questions.title: The parents of top athletes have often been successful athletes themselves.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词包括“parents”、“top athletes”和“successful athletes”。在原文中，最接近的表达是“choose his parents carefully”，但这只是一个比喻，强调基因的重要性，并没有具体提到运动员的父母是否也是成功的运动员。因此，文章中没有提供关于顶级运动员的父母是否也是成功运动员的信息，这就是为什么正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: The athlete must choose his parents carefully," says
central_sentences.translation: 运动员必须仔细选择他的父母。

Over the past century, the composition of the human gene pool has not changed appreciably, but with increasing global participation in athletics-and greater rewards to tempt athletes-it is more likely that individuals possessing the unique complement of genes for athletic performance can be identified early.

questions.title: The growing international importance of athletics means that gifted athletes can be recognised at a younger age.
questions.analysis: 这道题的答案是TRUE，因为文章中提到由于全球参与体育运动的增加，有天赋的运动员能够被及早发现。这与题干所述的意思是一致的。题干中的“growing international importance”对应原文中的“increasing global participation”，“gifted athletes”对应“individuals possessing the unique complement of genes for athletic performance”，“recognised at a younger age”对应“identified early”。

central_sentences.sentence: Over the past century, the composition of the human gene pool has not changed appreciably, but with increasing global participation in athletics-and greater rewards to tempt athletes-it is more likely that individuals possessing the unique complement of genes for athletic performance can be identified early.
central_sentences.translation: 在过去的一个世纪里，人类基因库的组成没有显著变化，但随着全球参与体育运动的增加和更大的奖励来吸引运动员，更有可能早期识别出具有独特运动基因组合的个人。

He will not predict by how much, however: "Exactly what the limits are it's hard to say, but there will be increases even if only by hundredths of a second, as long as our training continues to improve."

Questions 11-13

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.title: Biomechanics films are proving particularly useful because they enable trainers to

options.text: highlight areas for improvement in athletes
assess the fitness levels of athletes

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A，因为文章中明确提到生物力学的应用可以帮助教练指出运动员需要改进的地方。具体来说，Dapena提到通过分析运动员的动作，可以发现他们跑步速度不够快或者起跳时手臂使用不够充分，这正是“highlight areas for improvement”的意思。

central_sentences.sentence: By applying Newton's law to these motions, "we can say that this athlete's run is not fast enough; that this one is not using his arms strongly enough during take-off," says Dapena, who uses these methods to help high jumpers.
central_sentences.translation: 通过将牛顿定律应用于这些动作，"我们可以说这个运动员的跑步速度不够快；那个运动员在起跳时没有充分利用他的手臂，"Dapena说，他使用这些方法来帮助跳高运动员。

target_sen.target_sen: By applying Newton's law to these motions, "we can say that this athlete's run is not fast enough; that this one is not using his arms strongly enough during take-off," says Dapena, who uses these methods to help high jumpers.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是D，因为文章明确指出生物力学专家是通过分析来理解Fosbury flop的。题干中的"explain"对应文章中的"comprehending"，而"Biomechanics specialists"和"Fosbury flop"在题干和文章中都是一致的。因此，最直接的对应关系是D选项。

central_sentences.sentence: That understanding took the later analysis of biomechanics specialists. Who put their minds to comprehending something that was too complex and unorthodox ever to have been invented through their own mathematical simulations.
central_sentences.translation: 这种理解需要生物力学专家的后续分析。他们致力于理解一些过于复杂和非正统的东西，这些东西通过他们自己的数学模拟是无法发明的。

questions.title: John S. Raglin believes our current knowledge of athletics is
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是 B: basic。题干中的 "current knowledge" 对应原文中的 "our understanding"，而 "basic" 对应原文中的 "fundamental"。Raglin 认为我们对运动学的理解在很多情况下是基本的，这表明我们的知识还处于初级阶段。

central_sentences.sentence: "Core performance is not a simple or mundane thing of higher, faster, longer. So many variables enter into the equation, and our understanding in many cases is fundamental. We're got a long way to go."

target_sen.target_sen: So many variables enter into the equation, and our understanding in many cases is fundamental.

content.title: The Nature and Aims of Archaeology

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Archaeology is partly the discovery of treasures of the past, partly the work of the scientific analyst, partly the exercise of the creative imagination. It is toiling in the sun on an excavation in the Middle East, it is working with living Inuit in the snows of Alaska, and it is investigating the sewers of Roman Britain. But it is also the painstaking task of interpretation, so that we come to understand what these things mean for the human story. And it is the conservation of the world's cultural heritage against looting and careless harm.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Archaeology, then, is both a physical activity out in the field, and an intellectual pursuit in the study or laboratory. That is part of its great attraction. The rich mixture of danger and detective work has also made it the perfect vehicle for fiction writers and film-makers, from Agatha Christie with Murder in Mesopotamia to Steven Spielberg with Indiana Jones. However far from reality such portrayals are, they capture the essential truth that archaeology is an exciting quest - the quest for knowledge about ourselves and our past.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: But how does archaeology relate to other disciplines such as anthropology and history that are also concerned with the human story? Is archaeology itself a science? And what are the responsibilities of the archaeologist in today's world?

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Anthropology, at its broadest, is the study of humanity—our physical characteristics as animals and our unique non-biological characteristics that we call culture. Culture in this sense includes what the anthropologist, Edward Tylor, summarised in 1871 as "knowledge, beliefs, art, morals, custom and any other capabilities and habits acquired by man as a member of society". Anthropologists also use the term "culture" in a more restricted sense when they refer to the "culture" of a particular society, meaning the non-biological characteristics unique to that society, which distinguish it from other societies. Anthropology is thus a broad discipline—so broad that it is generally broken down into three smaller disciplines: physical anthropology, cultural anthropology and archaeology.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Physical anthropology, or biological anthropology as it is called, concerns the study of human biological or physical characteristics and how they evolved. Cultural anthropology—or social anthropology—analyses human culture and society. Two of its branches are ethnography (the study at first hand of individual living cultures) and ethnology (which sets out to compare cultures using ethnographic evidence to derive general principles about human society).

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Archaeology is the "past tense of cultural anthropology". Whereas cultural anthropologists will often base their conclusions on the experience of living within contemporary communities, archaeologists study past societies primarily through their material remains—the buildings, tools, and other artefacts that constitute what is known as the material culture left over from former societies.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Nevertheless, one of the most important tasks for the archaeologist today is to know how to interpret material culture in human terms. How were those pots used? Why are some dwellings round and others square? Here the methods of archaeology and ethnography overlap. Archaeologists in recent decades have developed "ethnoarchaeology", where, like ethnographers, they live among contemporary communities, but with the specific purpose of learning how such societies use material culture—how they make their tools and weapons, why they build their settlements where they do, and so on. Moreover, archaeology has a role to play in the field of conservation. Heritage studies constitute a developing field, where it is realised that the world's cultural heritage is a diminishing resource which holds different meanings for different people.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: If, then, archaeology deals with the past, in what way does it differ from history? In the broadest sense, just as archaeology is an aspect of anthropology, so too is it a part of history—where we mean the whole history of humankind from its beginnings over three million years ago. Indeed, for more than ninety-nine percent of that huge span of time, archaeology—the study of past material culture—is the only significant source of information. Conventional historical sources begin only with the introduction of written records around 3,000 BC in western Asia, and much later in most other parts of the world.

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: A commonly drawn distinction is between prehistory, i.e. the period before written records, and history in the narrow sense, meaning the study of the past using written evidence. To archaeology, which studies all cultures and periods, whether with or without writing, the distinction between history and prehistory is a convenient dividing line that recognises the importance of the written word, but in no way lessens the importance of the useful information contained in oral histories.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: Since the aim of archaeology is the understanding of humankind, it is a humanistic study, and since it deals with the human past, it is a historical discipline. But it differs from the study of written history in a fundamental way. The material the archaeologist finds does not tell us directly what to think. Historical records make statements, offer opinions and pass judgements. The objects the archaeologists discover, on the other hand, tell us nothing directly in themselves. In this respect, the practice of the archaeologist is rather like that of the scientist, who collects data, conducts experiments, formulates a hypothesis, tests the hypothesis against more data, and then, in conclusion, devises a model that seems best to summarise the pattern observed in the data. The archaeologist has to develop a picture of the past, just as the scientist has to develop a coherent view of the natural world.

groups.desc:
Questions 14-19

YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer

questions.title: Archaeology involves creativity as well as investigative work.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "creativity" 和 "investigative work" 在原文中分别对应 "creative imagination" 和 "discovery of treasures" 以及 "work of the scientific analyst"。原文明确指出考古学包含创造性想象和调查工作，因此答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: Archaeology is partly the discovery of treasures of the past, partly the work of the scientific analyst, partly the exercise of the creative imagination.
central_sentences.translation: 考古学部分是对过去宝藏的发现，部分是科学分析师的工作，部分是创造性想象的运用。

target_sen.target_sen: Archaeology is partly the discovery of treasures of the past, partly the work of the scientific analyst, partly the exercise of the creative imagination.
Archaeology is partly the discovery of treasures of the past, partly the work of the scientific analyst, partly the exercise of the creative imagination.
Archaeology is partly the discovery of treasures of the past, partly the work of the scientific analyst, partly the exercise of the creative imagination.

questions.title: Archaeologist must be able to translate texts from ancient languages.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是NOT GIVEN，因为文章中没有提供足够的信息来判断考古学家是否必须具备翻译古代语言文本的能力。文章讨论了考古学的性质、方法和与其他学科的关系，但并未特别提到翻译古代语言的要求。学生需要仔细阅读，并意识到没有找到相关信息也是一种答案。

central_sentences.reason: 文中未涉及语言翻译能力要求
central_sentences.analysis: 全文未提及考古学家需翻译古代文字。

questions.title: Movies give a realistic picture of the work of archaeologists.
questions.analysis: 题干中的"Movies give a realistic picture of the work of archaeologists"意思是电影给出了考古学家工作的真实画面。而原文中明确提到"However far from reality such portrayals are"，意思是这些描述与现实相去甚远。因此，题干中的说法是错误的，正确答案应为NO/FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: However far from reality such portrayals are, they capture the essential truth that archaeology is an exciting quest - the quest for knowledge about ourselves and our past.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，这些描述与现实相去甚远，但它们捕捉到了考古学是一个激动人心的探索这一本质真理——对我们自己和我们过去的知识的探索。

target_sen.target_sen: The rich mixture of danger and detective work has also made it the perfect vehicle for fiction writers and film-makers, from Agatha Christie with Murder in Mesopotamia to Stephen Spielberg with Indiana Jones.
However far from reality such portrayals are, they capture the essential truth that archaeology is an exciting quest - the quest for knowledge about ourselves and our past.
However far from reality such portrayals are, they capture the essential truth that archaeology is an exciting quest - the quest for knowledge about ourselves and our past.

questions.title: Anthropologist define culture in more than one way.
questions.analysis:
嘿呀，这题答案确实是YES哈，老师之前给的解析里结论说错啦，应该是答案为YES，而不是NO。
咱们来好好瞅瞅文章哈。文中提到人类学家对“culture”有不同的定义方式。一方面，按照人类学家Edward Tylor在1871年的总结，文化是“知识、信仰、艺术、道德、习俗以及人类作为社会成员所获得的任何其他能力和习惯”，这是一个广义的定义。另一方面呢，人类学家在提及特定社会的“文化”时，会用更狭义的方式，指的是该社会独有的、区别于其他社会的非生物特征。
你看，这里很明显人类学家对“culture”有不止一种定义方式呀，所以题干说“Anthropologists define culture in more than one way”是正确的，答案就是YES

central_sentences.sentence: Anthropologists also use the term "culture" in a more restricted sense when they refer to the "culture" of a particular society, meaning the non-biological characteristics unique to that society, which distinguish it from other societies. Anthropology
central_sentences.translation: 人类学家在提到特定社会的“文化”时，也会以更狭义的方式使用“文化”这个词，指的是该社会独特的非生物特征，这些特征将其与其他社会区分开来。

target_sen.target_sen: Anthropologists also use the term "culture" in a more restricted sense when they refer to the "culture" of a particular society, meaning the non-biological characteristics unique to that society, which distinguish it from other societies.

Anthropologists also use the term "culture" in a more restricted sense when they refer to the "culture" of a particular society, meaning the non-biological characteristics unique to that society, which distinguish it from other societies.
Anthropology, at its broadest, is the study of humanity- our physical characteristics as animals and our unique non-biological characteristics that we call culture.

Anthropologists also use the term "culture" in a more restricted sense when they refer to the "culture" of a particular society, meaning the non-biological characteristics unique to that society, which distinguish it from other societies.

questions.title: Archaeology is a more demanding field of study than anthropology.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Archaeology is a more demanding field of study than anthropology”在文章中没有找到直接的对应句子。文章虽然详细描述了考古学的多个方面和复杂性，但并没有与人类学进行直接比较。因此，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。这种题型要求学生严格根据文章给出的信息判断，不要过度推断或使用背景知识。

central_sentences.analysis: 文中未比较考古学与人类学的难度。

questions.title: The history of Europe has been documented since 3,000 BC.
questions.analysis:
答案是N，不过解析里“西亚可以被广义地理解为包括欧洲的一部分”这个说法有误哈。
具体分析
原文提到 “Conventional historical sources begin only with the introduction of written records around 3,000 BC in western Asia, and much later in most other parts in the world.”，意思是传统的历史资料是从公元前3000年左右西亚出现书面记录开始的，而世界上大多数其他地区的书面记录出现得要晚得多。
题干说 “The history of Europe has been documented since 3,000 BC.”（自公元前3000年起，欧洲的历史就有文献记载了）。从原文可知公元前3000年有书面记录的是西亚，并非欧洲，所以题干信息与原文信息相悖，答案应该是NO。

central_sentences.sentence: Conventional historical sources begin only with the introduction of written records around 3,000 BC in western Asia
central_sentences.translation: 传统的历史资料仅从公元前3000年左右在西亚引入书面记录开始。

target_sen.target_sen: Conventional historical sources begin only with the introduction of written records around 3,000 BC in western Asia, and much later in most other parts in the world.
Conventional historical sources begin only with the introduction of written records around 3,000 BC in western Asia, and much later in most other parts in the world.

groups.title: Which TWO statements are mentioned by the writer of the text?
groups.desc:
Questions 20 and 21

Choose TWO letters A - E.
The list below gives some statements about anthropology.

collect_option.title: Which TWO statements are mentioned by the writer of the text?

list.text: It is important for government planners
It is a continually growing field of study
It often involves long periods of fieldwork
It is subdivided for study purposes
It studies human evolutionary patterns

questions.analysis: 这道题目要求考生找出关于人类学的两个正确陈述。通过定位题干中的线索词“subdivided”和“evolutionary patterns”，可以在原文中找到对应的同义替换词“broken down”和“how they evolved”。这些词语帮助我们定位到答案句，从而确定正确答案为D和E。

central_sentences.sentence: Anthropology is thus a broad discipline - so broad that it is generally broken down into three smaller disciplines
central_sentences.translation: 人类学因此是一门广泛的学科——如此广泛，以至于通常被分为三个较小的学科。

central_sentences.sentence: Physical anthropology, or biological anthropology as it is called, concerns the study of human biological or physical characteristics and how they evolved
central_sentences.translation: 体质人类学，或称生物人类学，关注人类生物或物理特征及其进化方式的研究。

central_sentences.sentence: Anthropology is thus a broad discipline - so broad that it is generally broken down into three smaller disciplines
central_sentences.translation: 人类学因此是一门广泛的学科——如此广泛，以至于通常被分为三个较小的学科。

central_sentences.sentence: Physical anthropology, or biological anthropology as it is called, concerns the study of human biological or physical characteristics and how they evolved
central_sentences.translation: 体质人类学，或称生物人类学，关注人类生物或物理特征及其进化方式的研究。

groups.title: Which TWO of these tasks are mentioned by the writer of the text?
groups.desc:
Questions 22 and 23

Choose TWO letters A - E.
Write your answer in boxes 22 and 23 on your answer sheet.

list.text: examining ancient waste sites to investigate diet
studying cave art to determine its significance
deducing reasons for the shape of domestic buildings
investigating the way different cultures make and use objects
examining evidence for past climate changes

questions.analysis: 这道题目要求考生从文章中找出考古学家所从事的具体任务。通过定位题干中的线索词并在文章中找到对应的同义替换词，可以确定正确答案为C和D。

groups.desc:
Questions 24-27

Complete the summary of the last two paragraphs of Reading Passage 2.
Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
groups.collect: Much of the work of archaeologists can be done using written records, but they find $34302$ equally valuable. The writer describes archaeology as both a $34303$ and a $34304$. However, as archaeologists do not try to influence human behaviour, the writer compares their style of working to that of a $34305$.

questions.analysis: 题干中的"written records"在原文中对应"written word"，"equally valuable"对应"in no way lessens the importance"。原文明确指出，尽管书面文字很重要，但口述历史中的有用信息同样重要。因此，答案是"oral histories"。

central_sentences.sentence: To archaeology, which studies all cultures and periods, whether with or without writing, the distinction between history and pre-history is a convenient dividing line that recognises the importance of the written word, but in no way lessens the importance of the useful information contained in oral histories.
central_sentences.translation: 对于考古学来说，它研究所有文化和时期，无论是否有文字记录，历史和史前史之间的区分是一个方便的分界线，它承认书面文字的重要性，但绝不减少口述历史中有用信息的重要性。

target_sen.target_sen: To archaeology, which studies all cultures and periods, whether with or without writing, the distinction between history and pre-history is a convenient dividing line that recognises the importance of the written word, but in no way lessens the importance of the useful information contained in oral histories.

syn_ary.synonym: in no way lessens the importance

target_sen.target_sen: To archaeology, which studies all cultures and periods, whether with or without writing, the distinction between history and pre-history is a convenient dividing line that recognises the importance of the written word, but in no way lessens the importance of the useful information contained in oral histories.

questions.analysis: 题目要求描述考古学的性质，文章中明确提到考古学是一门“humanistic study”（人文学科），这与题目中的描述直接对应。因此，正确答案是“humanistic study”。

central_sentences.sentence: Since the aim of archaeology is the understanding of humankind, it is a humanistic study, and since it deals with the human past, it is a historical discipline.
central_sentences.translation: 由于考古学的目标是理解人类，因此它是一门人文学科；并且由于它处理的是人类的过去，因此它是一门历史学科。

target_sen.target_sen: Since the aim of archaeology is the understanding of humankind, it is a humanistic study, and since it deals with the human past, it is a historical discipline.
But is differs from the study of written history in a fundamental way.
The material the archaeologist finds does not tell us directly what to think.
Historical records make statements, offer opinions and pass judgements.
The objects the archaeologists discover, on the other hand, tell us nothing directly in themselves.

In this respect, the practice of the archaeologist is rather like that of the scientist, who collects data, conducts experiments, formulates a hypothesis tests the hypothesis against more data, and then, in conclusion, devises a model that seems best to summarise the pattern observed in the data.
The archaeologist has to develop a picture of the past, just as the scientist has to develop a coherent view of the natural world.

questions.analysis: 题干中的关键词 "describes", "archaeology", "both", 和 "and" 在原文中直接对应的句子是 "Since the aim of archaeology is the understanding of humankind, it is a humanistic study, and since it deals with the human past, it is a historical discipline." 这句话明确指出考古学既是一门人文学科（humanistic study），也是一门历史学科（historical discipline）。因此，正确答案是 "historical discipline"。

Since the aim of archaeology is the understanding of humankind, it is a humanistic study, and since it deals with the human past, it is a historical discipline.

content.title: The Problem of Scarce Resources
content.summary: {'A': '经济发达国家的每个卫生系统都面临着如何以最公正和最有效的方式分配医疗资源的问题，包括资源分配比例、优先事项等。', 'B': '20 世纪 50 年代起，人们对整体资源特别是医疗资源的有限性以及相关具体方面的看法有了新变化。', 'C': '西方社会同时出现了人们有获得医疗保健作为适当人类生活必要条件的基本权利的意识。', 'D': '大多数社会认识到人们有获得医疗保健的权利，国家有义务确保提供足够的公共医疗资源。', 'E': '当医疗资源明显无法满足需求时，人们要求国家满足其基本医疗权利，同时卫生成本大幅上升等带来了对医疗资源分配的担忧。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The problem of how health-care resources should be allocated or apportioned, so that they are distributed in both the most just and most efficient way, is not a new one. Every health system in an economically developed society is faced with the need to decide (either formally or informally) what proportion of the community's total resources should be spent on health-care; how resources are to be apportioned; what diseases and disabilities and which forms of treatment are to be given priority; which members of the community are to be given special consideration in respect of their health needs; and which forms of treatment are the most cost-effective.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: What is new is that, from the 1950s onwards, there have been certain general changes in outlook about the finitude of resources as a whole and of health-care resources in particular, as well as more specific changes regarding the clientele of health-care resources and the cost to the community of those resources. Thus, in the 1950s and 1960s, there emerged an awareness in Western societies that resources for the provision of fossil fuel energy were finite and exhaustible and that the capacity of nature or the environment to sustain economic development and population was also finite. In other words, we became aware of the obvious fact that there were "limits to growth". The new consciousness that there were also severe limits to health-care resources was part of this general revelation of the obvious. Looking back, it now seems quite incredible that in the national health systems that emerged in many countries in the years immediately after the 1939-45 World War, it was assumed without question that all the basic health needs of any community could be satisfied, at least in principle; the "invisible hand" of economic progress would provide.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: However, at exactly the same time as this new realization of the finite character of health-care resources was sinking in, an awareness of a contrary kind was developing in Western societies: that people have a basic right to health-care as a necessary condition of a proper human life. Like education, political and legal processes and institutions, public order, communication, transport and money supply, health-care came to be seen as one of the fundamental social facilities necessary for people to exercise their other rights as autonomous human beings. People are not in a position to exercise personal liberty and to be self-determining if they are poverty-stricken, or deprived of basic education, or do not live within a context of law and order. In the same way, basic health-care is a condition of the exercise of autonomy.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Although the language of "rights" sometimes leads to confusion, by the late 1970s it was recognized in most societies that people have a right to health-care (though there has been considerable resistance in the United States to the idea that there is a formal right to health-care). It is also accepted that this right generates an obligation or duty for the state to ensure that adequate health-care resources are provided out of the public purse. The state has no obligation to provide a health-care system itself, but to ensure that such a system is provided. Put another way, basic health-care is now recognized as a "public good", rather than a "private good" that one is expected to buy for oneself. As the 1976 declaration of the World Health Organization put it: "The enjoyment of the highest attainable standard of health is one of the fundamental rights of every human being without distinction of race, religion, political belief, economic or social condition". As has just been remarked, in a liberal society basic health is seen as one of the indispensable conditions for the exercise of personal autonomy.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text:
Just at the time when it became obvious that health-care resources could not possibly meet the demands being made upon them, people were demanding that their fundamental right to health-care be satisfied by the state. The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources. Thus in OECD countries as a whole, health costs increased from 3.8% of GDP in 1960 to 7% of GDP in 1980, and it has been predicted that the proportion of health costs to GDP will continue to increase. (In the US the current figure is about 12% of GDP, and in Australia about 7.8% of GDP.)

As a consequence, during the 1980s a kind of doomsday scenario (analogous to similar doomsday extrapolations about energy needs and fossil fuels or about population increases) was projected by health administrators, economists and politicians. In this scenario, ever-rising health costs were matched against static or declining resources.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 28-31

Reading Passage 3 has five sections A-E.
Choose the correct heading for section A and C-E from the list of headings below.

Example Answer
Section B viii - Sustainable economic development

list.text: The connection between health-care and other human rights
The development of market-based health systems.
The role of the state in health-care
A problem shared by every economically developed country
The impact of recent change
The views of the medical establishment
The end of an illusion
Sustainable economic development

questions.analysis: Section A 讨论了在每一个经济发达国家中，如何分配医疗资源的问题。这与标题 iv "A problem shared by every economically developed country" 完全吻合，因为段落中明确提到这是所有经济发达社会面临的共同问题。

central_sentences.sentence: Every health system in an economically developed society is faced with the need to decide (either formally or informally) what proportion of the community's total resources should be spent on health-care
central_sentences.translation: 每一个经济发达社会的卫生系统都面临着需要决定（无论是正式还是非正式地）社区总资源中有多少比例应该花在医疗保健上的问题...

syn_ary.synonym: every health system in an economically developed society

target_sen.target_sen: Every health system in an economically developed society is faced with the need to decide (either formally or informally) what proportion of the community's total resources should be spent on health-care; how resources are to be apportioned; what diseases and disabilities and which forms of treatment are to be given priority; which members of the community are to be given special consideration in respect of their health needs; and which forms of treatment are the most cost-effective.

questions.analysis: Section C 讨论了健康医疗如何成为人们行使其他权利的必要条件。文章将健康医疗与教育、法律程序等并列，称之为“fundamental social facilities”，这些都是人们行使其他权利所必需的。通过理解这一点，学生就能确定 i 是最合适的标题。

central_sentences.sentence: health-care came to be seen as one of the fundamental social facilities necessary for people to exercise their other rights as autonomous human beings.
central_sentences.translation: 健康医疗被视为人们行使其他权利所必需的基本社会设施之一。

syn_ary.synonym: came to be seen as one of the fundamental social facilities necessary for people to exercise their other rights

target_sen.target_sen: Like education, political and legal processes and institutions, public order, communication, transport and money supply, health-care came to be seen as one of the fundamental social facilities necessary for people to exercise their other rights as autonomous human beings.

However, at exactly the same time as this new realization of the finite character of health-care resources was sinking in, an awareness of a contrary kind was developing in Western societies: that people have a basic right to health-care as a necessary condition of a proper human life.

Like education, political and legal processes and institutions, public order, communication, transport and money supply, health-care came to be seen as one of the fundamental social facilities necessary for people to exercise their other rights as autonomous human beings.
In the same way, basic health-care is a condition of the exercise of autonomy.

Like education, political and legal processes and institutions, public order, communication, transport and money supply, health-care came to be seen as one of the fundamental social facilities necessary for people to exercise their other rights as autonomous human beings.

questions.analysis: Section D 主要讨论了国家在医疗保健中的角色和责任。段落中明确提到国家的义务是确保医疗保健系统的提供，而不是自己提供。这与题目中的 "The role of the state in health-care" 完全对应。

central_sentences.sentence: The state has no obligation to provide a health-care system itself, but to ensure that such a system is provided.
central_sentences.translation: 国家没有义务自己提供医疗保健系统，但有义务确保这样的系统被提供。

target_sen.target_sen: It is also accepted that this right generates an obligation or duty for the state to ensure that adequate health-care resources are provided out of the public purse.
The state has no obligation to provide a health-care system itself, but to ensure that such a system is provided.
It is also accepted that this right generates an obligation or duty for the state to ensure that adequate health-care resources are provided out of the public purse.

It is also accepted that this right generates an obligation or duty for the state to ensure that adequate health-care resources are provided out of the public purse.

questions.analysis: Section E 讨论了最近的变化对医疗资源分配的影响，包括医疗成本的上升和人口结构的变化。这些变化导致了对医疗资源分配的关注。因此，"The impact of recent change" 是最合适的小标题。

central_sentences.sentence: Just at the time when it became obvious that health-care resources could not possibly meet the demands being made upon them, people were demanding that their fundamental right to health-care be satisfied by the state. The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.
central_sentences.translation: 就在医疗资源显然无法满足需求的时候，人们要求国家满足他们的基本医疗权利。导致当前对医疗资源分配关注的第二组更具体的变化源于大多数经合组织国家医疗成本的急剧上升，伴随着大规模的人口和社会变化，这意味着，例如，老年人现在是主要的（且相对非常昂贵的）医疗资源消费者。

target_sen.target_sen: The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.

The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.

groups.desc:
Questions 32-35
Classify the following as first occurring

list.text: between 1945 and 1950
between 1950 and 1980
after 1980

questions.title: the realisation that the resources of the national health system were limited
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B "between 1950 and 1980"，因为文章在Section B中明确提到“in the 1950s and 1960s, there emerged an awareness... that resources... were finite and exhaustible”，这与题干中的“the realisation that the resources of the national health system were limited”相对应。题干中的“realisation”对应文章中的“awareness”，“limited”对应“finite”和“exhaustible”。

central_sentences.sentence: Thus, in the 1950s and 1960s, there emerged an awareness in Western societies that resources for the provision of fossil fuel energy were finite and exhaustible and that the capacity of nature or the environment to sustain economic development and population was also finite.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，在20世纪50年代和60年代，西方社会开始意识到化石燃料能源的供应是有限和可耗尽的，自然或环境维持经济发展和人口的能力也是有限的。

target_sen.target_sen: Thus, in the 1950s and 1960s, there emerged an awareness in Western societies that resources for the provision of fossil fuel energy were finite and exhaustible and that the capacity of nature or the environment to sustain economic development and population was also finite.

Thus, in the 1950s and 1960s, there emerged an awareness in Western societies that resources for the provision of fossil fuel energy were finite and exhaustible and that the capacity of nature or the environment to sustain economic development and population was also finite.

Thus, in the 1950s and 1960s, there emerged an awareness in Western societies that resources for the provision of fossil fuel energy were finite and exhaustible and that the capacity of nature or the environment to sustain economic development and population was also finite.

questions.title: a sharp rise in the cost of health-care.
questions.analysis: 正确答案是B (between 1950 and 1980)，因为文章在Section E中明确指出，在1960到1980年间，医疗成本显著上升。这段时间属于1950-1980这个时期。

central_sentences.sentence: Thus in OECD countries as a whole, health costs increased from 3.8% of GDP in 1960 to 7% of GDP in 1980, and it has been predicted that the proportion of health costs to GDP will continue to increase.
central_sentences.translation: 因此，在整个经合组织国家中，医疗成本从1960年的占GDP的3.8%增加到1980年的7%，并且预计医疗成本占GDP的比例将继续增加。

target_sen.target_sen: The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.

The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.

Thus in OECD countries as a whole, health costs increased from 3.8% of GDP in 1960 to 7% of GDP in 1980, and it has been predicted that the proportion of health costs to GDP will continue to increase.
In this scenario, ever-rising health costs were matched against static or declining resources.

questions.title: a belief that all the health-care resources the community needed would be produced by economic growth
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是A "between 1945 and 1950"，因为文章明确指出这种信念存在于二战后立即出现的国家卫生系统中。这个时间段正好对应1945-1950年间。文章后面提到，从1950年代开始，人们才意识到资源是有限的，这与题干所述的信念相反。因此，这种信念必然是在1950年之前存在的。

central_sentences.sentence: it was assumed without question that all the basic health needs of any community could be satisfied, at least in principle; the "in visible hand" of economic progress would provide
central_sentences.translation: 毫无疑问地假设，任何社区的所有基本健康需求至少在原则上都可以得到满足；经济进步的“看不见的手”会提供这些资源。

central_sentences.sentence: in the national health systems that emerged in many countries in the years immediately after the 1939-45 World War
central_sentences.translation: 在1939-45年世界大战后立即出现在许多国家的国家卫生系统中

target_sen.target_sen: Looking back, it now seems quite incredible that in the national health systems that emerged in many countries in the years immediately after the 1939-45 World War, it was assumed without question that all the basic health needs of any community could be satisfied, at least in principle; the "in visible hand" of economic progress would provide.

Looking back, it now seems quite incredible that in the national health systems that emerged in many countries in the years immediately after the 1939-45 World War, it was assumed without question that all the basic health needs of any community could be satisfied, at least in principle; the "in visible hand" of economic progress would provide.

Looking back, it now seems quite incredible that in the national health systems that emerged in many countries in the years immediately after the 1939-45 World War, it was assumed without question that all the basic health needs of any community could be satisfied, at least in principle; the "in visible hand" of economic progress would provide.

Looking back, it now seems quite incredible that in the national health systems that emerged in many countries in the years immediately after the 1939-45 World War, it was assumed without question that all the basic health needs of any community could be satisfied, at least in principle; the "in visible hand" of economic progress would provide.

Looking back, it now seems quite incredible that in the national health systems that emerged in many countries in the years immediately after the 1939-45 World War, it was assumed without question that all the basic health needs of any community could be satisfied, at least in principle; the "in visible hand" of economic progress would provide.

Looking back, it now seems quite incredible that in the national health systems that emerged in many countries in the years immediately after the 1939-45 World War, it was assumed without question that all the basic health needs of any community could be satisfied, at least in principle; the "in visible hand" of economic progress would provide.

questions.title: an acceptance of the role of the state in guaranteeing the provision of health-care.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是B（1950-1980），因为在Section D中明确提到“by the late 1970s it was recognized in most societies that people have a right to health-care”，并且提到了国家的义务。这一时间段正好落在1950-1980之间。

central_sentences.sentence: It is also accepted that this right generates an obligation or duty for the state to ensure that adequate health-care resources are provided out of the public purse.
central_sentences.translation: 人们也接受了这一权利会产生国家的义务或责任，以确保从公共资金中提供足够的医疗资源。

syn_ary.synonym: obligation or duty for the state

target_sen.target_sen: It is also accepted that this right generates an obligation or duty for the state to ensure that adequate health-care resources are provided out of the public purse.

syn_ary.synonym: ensure that adequate health-care resources are provided

target_sen.target_sen: It is also accepted that this right generates an obligation or duty for the state to ensure that adequate health-care resources are provided out of the public purse.

groups.desc:
Questions 36-40

Do the following statements agree with the view of the writer in Reading Passage 3?
Choose
YES if the statement agrees with the views of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the views of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.title: Personal liberty and independence have never been regarded as directly linked to health-care.
questions.analysis: 答案是NO，因为文章中明确提到基本医疗保健是行使个人自由和自主权的一个条件。这表明个人自由和独立性与医疗保健之间是直接相关的，而题干中说“从未被视为直接相关”是错误的。

central_sentences.sentence: People are not in a position to exercise personal liberty and to be self-determining if they are poverty-stricken, or deprived of basic education, or do not live within a context of law and order. In the same way, basic health-care is a condition of the exercise of autonomy.
central_sentences.translation: 如果人们贫困、缺乏基本教育或不生活在法治环境中，他们就无法行使个人自由和自主决定权。同样，基本医疗保健是行使自主权的一个条件。

target_sen.target_sen: People are not in a position to exercise personal liberty and to be self-determining if they are poverty-stricken, or deprived of basic education, or do not live within a context of law and order.
People are not in a position to exercise personal liberty and to be self-determining if they are poverty-stricken, or deprived of basic education, or do not live within a context of law and order.

However, at exactly the same time as this new realization of the finite character of health-care resources was sinking in, an awareness of a contrary kind was developing in Western societies: that people have a basic right to health-care as a necessary condition of a proper human life.

Like education, political and legal processes and institutions, public order, communication, transport and money supply, health-care came to be seen as one of the fundamental social facilities necessary for people to exercise their other rights as autonomous human beings.
In the same way, basic health-care is a condition of the exercise of autonomy.

questions.title: Health-care came to be seen as a right at about the same time that the limits of health-care resources became evident.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Health-care came to be seen as a right at about the same time that the limits of health-care resources became evident”在原文中得到了明确的对应。原文中提到“at exactly the same time as this new realization of the finite character of health-care resources was sinking in, an awareness of a contrary kind was developing... that people have a basic right to health-care”，这表明在意识到医疗资源有限的同时，人们也开始认为医疗保健是一项基本权利。因此，答案是YES。

central_sentences.sentence: However, at exactly the same time as this new realization of the finite character of health-care resources was sinking in, an awareness of a contrary kind was developing in Western societies: that people have a basic right to health-care as a necessary condition of a proper human life.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，正当人们逐渐意识到医疗资源的有限性时，西方社会也在发展一种相反的意识：人们有权享有基本的医疗保健，作为过上正常人生活的必要条件。

target_sen.target_sen: However, at exactly the same time as this new realization of the finite character of health-care resources was sinking in, an awareness of a contrary kind was developing in Western societies: that people have a basic right to health-care as a necessary condition of a proper human life.

Like education, political and legal processes and institutions, public order, communication, transport and money supply, health-care came to be seen as one of the fundamental social facilities necessary for people to exercise their other rights as autonomous human beings.
In the same way, basic health-care is a condition of the exercise of autonomy.

However, at exactly the same time as this new realization of the finite character of health-care resources was sinking in, an awareness of a contrary kind was developing in Western societies: that people have a basic right to health-care as a necessary condition of a proper human life.

questions.title: IN OECD countries population changes have had an impact on health-care costs in recent years.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是YES，因为题干中的"population changes"在原文中对应的是"demographic changes"，而"impact on health-care costs"对应的是"very expensive consumers of health-care resources"。原文明确提到人口变化（尤其是老年人）导致了医疗资源的高成本，因此题干中的信息在文章中得到了验证。

central_sentences.sentence: large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.
central_sentences.translation: 大规模的人口和社会变化意味着，例如，老年人现在是主要的（且相对非常昂贵的）医疗资源消费者。

syn_ary.synonym: very expensive consumers of health-care resources

target_sen.target_sen: The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.

questions.title: OECD governments have consistently underestimated the level of health-care provision needed.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词 "OECD governments" 在原文中对应的是 "OECD countries"，"health-care provision needed" 对应的是 "health-care resources"。然而，题干中的 "underestimated" 在原文中没有找到直接的同义替换词。原文提到的是医疗成本的上升和资源的有限性，但并没有明确提到政府低估了所需的医疗服务水平。因此，正确答案是 NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: In this scenario, ever-rising health costs were matched against static or declining resources.
central_sentences.translation: 在这种情景中，不断上升的医疗成本与静态或下降的资源相匹配。

target_sen.target_sen: The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.

Thus in OECD countries as a whole, health costs increased from 3.8% of GDP in 1960 to 7% of GDP in 1980, and it has been predicted that the proportion of health costs to GDP will continue to increase.

Just at the time when it became obvious that health-care resources could not possibly meet the demands being made upon them, people were demanding that their fundamental right to health-care be satisfied by the state.

The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.

questions.title: In most economically developed countries the elderly will to make special provision for their health-care in the future.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息是“在大多数经济发达国家，老年人将来需要为他们的医疗保健做特别安排”。文章中提到老年人是医疗资源的主要消费者，但没有提到他们将来需要为自己的医疗保健做特别安排。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到，正确答案是NOT GIVEN。

central_sentences.sentence: elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources
central_sentences.translation: 老年人现在是医疗资源的主要（且相对非常昂贵的）消费者。

target_sen.target_sen: The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.

Just at the time when it became obvious that health-care resources could not possibly meet the demands being made upon them, people were demanding that their fundamental right to health-care be satisfied by the state.

The second set of more specific changes that have led to the present concern about the distribution of health-care resources stems from the dramatic rise in health costs in most OECD countries, accompanied by large-scale demographic and social changes which have meant, to take one example, that elderly people are now major (and relatively very expensive) consumers of health-care resources.
